title: Property, housing and estate managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image or raise issue awareness for their organization or client. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities to solicit and maintain funds for special projects or nonprofit organizations. Plan, direct, or coordinate one or more administrative services of an organization, such as records and information management, mail distribution, and other office support services. (Based on 44 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Present purchase offers to sellers for consideration, act as an intermediary in negotiations between buyers and sellers, and accompany buyers during visits to and inspections of properties, advising them on suitability and value.

Inspect the condition of premises and arrange for necessary maintenance or notify owners of maintenance needs. Answer clients' questions regarding construction work, financing, maintenance, repairs, and appraisals, and review plans for new construction with clients, recommending available options and features.
Coordinate appointments to show homes to prospective buyers and display commercial, industrial, agricultural, and residential properties to clients, explaining their features.
Compare properties with similar ones that have recently sold to determine competitive market prices, and generate lists of properties compatible with buyers' needs and financial resources.
Promote property sales through advertisements, open houses, and participation in multiple listing services; coordinate property closings, overseeing signing of documents and disbursement of funds.

Prepare documents such as representation contracts, purchase agreements, closing statements, deeds, and leases, and maintain knowledge of real estate law, local economies, fair housing laws, types of available mortgages, financing options, and government programs.

Act as consultants to agency staff and other community programs regarding federal, state, and county regulations and policies; and meet with boards of directors and committees to discuss program-related issues.
Rent properties or manage rental properties, and advise sellers on how to make homes more appealing to potential buyers.
Investigate complaints, disturbances, and violations, resolving problems in accordance with management rules and regulations, and maintain a safe, secure, and well-maintained environment.

Meet with clients to negotiate management and service contracts, determine priorities, and discuss the financial and operational status of properties; and maintain records of sales, rental or usage activity, maintenance costs, and property availability.
Prepare budgets and financial reports for properties, and oversee operations, maintenance, administration, and improvement of commercial, industrial, or residential properties.
Direct activities of professional and technical staff members and volunteers, and evaluate their performance to ensure program effectiveness.

Establish and maintain relationships with agencies and organizations within the community to meet needs and ensure services are not duplicated; and represent organizations in relations with governmental and media institutions.
Conduct research and analyze community needs to determine program directions and goals, and discuss and resolve organizational policies on program benefits and requirements.
Advise clients on market conditions, prices, mortgages, legal requirements, and related matters; and evaluate mortgage options to help clients obtain financing.

Maintain awareness of current income tax regulations, local zoning, building and tax laws, and the potential for growth in property areas; and maintain contact with insurance carriers, fire and police departments to ensure compliance with codes and regulations.
Review existing standards, controls, or equipment and recommend necessary changes or upgrades; and prepare and maintain records, such as budgets and personnel records, to ensure effective management.
Contact property owners to advertise services and solicit property sales listings, and maintain contact with previous clients for prospecting referral business.
Direct fundraising activities and the preparation of public relations materials.
Evaluate properties and conduct seminars for sales agents to improve techniques.
Conduct seminars for sales agents to improve sales techniques and negotiate property purchase financing.
Secure and manage construction or purchase financing, and oversee the administration of contracts for service provision.
Analyze proposed legislation and regulations to determine impacts on agency services; and gather information from surveys, reports, and legal records.
Clean common areas, change light bulbs, and make minor property repairs. Plan general maintenance, major repairs, and remodeling projects.
Arrange meetings between buyers and sellers for negotiations and ensure that rental and advertising practices comply with regulations.

Coordinate security operations or activities with public law enforcement, fire and other agencies; conduct threat or vulnerability analyses to assess risks from disasters or criminal activity; conduct physical examinations of property for compliance with security policies; and communicate security status, updates, and problems using established protocols.

Plan, administer, and control budgets for contracts, equipment, and supplies; prepare and review operational reports and schedules for accuracy and efficiency; and develop cost reduction strategies and savings plans.

Participate in architectural and engineering planning and design, including space and installation management; oversee construction and renovation projects to improve efficiency and ensure compliance with standards; and develop and implement plans for facility modification or expansion.

Attend meetings, professional seminars, or conferences to stay updated on changes in directives or technologies impacting security operations; assess risks to mitigate potential consequences and develop incident response plans; and develop, recommend, or manage security procedures for operations.

Analyze and evaluate security operations through auditing and assessments to identify risks or opportunities for improvement; monitor and ensure ethical compliance in operations; and identify, investigate, or resolve security breaches.
Direct or participate in emergency management and contingency planning; develop and implement security protocols for facilities and personnel; and oversee maintenance and repairs of security systems.

Train subordinate security professionals in security rules and procedures; supervise or provide leadership to staff including hiring, training, and performance evaluation; and prepare employee work schedules and oversee attendance for payroll purposes.

Prepare reports or presentations on investigations, operational issues, and compliance with regulations; monitor security policies and ensure compliance with internal and governmental requirements; and communicate with external vendors to meet organizational needs.
Plan security for special and high-risk events; oversee security activities to safeguard individuals on company property; and conduct classes to teach procedures to staff.

Develop or manage investigation programs, including the collection and preservation of evidence; write or review security-related documents such as proposals and strategic initiatives; and prepare reports regarding market conditions and service effectiveness for internal use.

Develop fundraising activity plans that maximize participation or contributions and minimize costs, and establish goals for soliciting funds, develop policies for collection and safeguarding of contributions, and coordinate disbursement of funds.
Establish and maintain effective working relationships with clients, government officials, and media representatives, and use these relationships to develop new fundraising opportunities.

Conduct research to identify the goals, net worth, charitable donation history, or other data related to potential donors or general donor markets, and develop strategies to encourage new or increased contributions.
Monitor suppliers' activities to assess performance in meeting quality or delivery requirements, and negotiate prices and terms with suppliers, vendors, or freight forwarders.
Lead, or participate in, fraud investigation teams, and conduct in-depth investigations of suspicious financial activity, such as suspected money-laundering efforts.
Prepare and maintain a directory of suppliers, contractors, and subcontractors, and set up cost monitoring and reporting systems and procedures.
Prepare estimates for use in selecting vendors or subcontractors, and consult with clients, vendors, and personnel in other departments to discuss and formulate estimates and resolve issues.

Prepare promotional plans, sales literature, media kits, and sales contracts, and write interesting and effective press releases, prepare information for media kits, and develop and maintain company internet or intranet web pages.

Conduct special studies to develop and establish standard hour and related cost data or to reduce cost, and analyze blueprints and other documentation to prepare time, cost, materials, and labor estimates.
Confer with clients to develop strategies for their careers, and to explain actions taken on their behalf, and schedule promotional or performance engagements for clients.
Maintain documentation of all loss prevention activity, including theft and fraud investigations.
Explain rules and regulations related to facilities and attractions to customers, ensuring compliance with all standards.
Develop and maintain partnerships with law enforcement agencies and members of the retail loss prevention community to enhance security measures.
Coordinate internal investigations of issues like employee theft and violations of loss prevention policies, and implement strategic loss prevention measures.
Collaborate with law enforcement to address external theft or fraud cases and investigate incidents of shoplifting or internal theft.
Assess security needs across locations and ensure proper deployment of loss prevention resources, including technology and staff.
Analyze retail data to identify trends in theft or fraud, and evaluate the effectiveness of loss prevention strategies.
Supervise surveillance and detection efforts related to theft and manage criminal processing protocols.
Calculate and record financial performance metrics such as department expenses and revenue.
Verify the correct use and maintenance of physical security systems like CCTV and burglar alarms to ensure compliance with safety standards.
Visit facilities to ensure compliance with company policies and procedures, addressing any issues that arise.
Provide recommendations and solutions in crisis situations, such as handling workplace violence or public demonstrations.
Interview and hire staff to fill vacancies, ensuring alignment with organizational goals.
Plan and direct schedules and programs for events and activities, aligning with strategic goals and budgetary constraints.
Prepare and implement budgets for operational and strategic initiatives, ensuring effective resource allocation.
Identify and apply for project funding while maintaining documentation of compliance activities and responding to regulatory agency requests.
Manage compliance to operational, safety, or inventory control procedures and conduct periodic internal reviews or audits to ensure adherence.
Provide training for staff on compliance-related topics, policies, or procedures to ensure understanding and adherence to standards.
Resolve customer inquiries or complaints, ensuring quality service and adherence to performance standards.
Communicate regulatory information and ensure personnel are adequately informed about compliance systems and policies.
Monitor regulatory trends and effective compliance to ensure alignment with industry standards and company operations.
Conduct training sessions or facilitate seminars for wellness and fitness staff, as well as develop staff service goals.
Supervise and manage programs that promote health, fitness, or wellness, ensuring safety and compliance with standards.
Document compliance activities and engage in continuing education to stay updated on industry developments, regulations, and best practices.
Develop and implement marketing strategies to enhance services or products, aligning with organizational goals.
Monitor the effectiveness of programs or services, evaluating their impact on organizational performance and compliance.
Review or evaluate environmental remediation project proposals and designs for contaminant treatment or disposal facilities.
Provide training on hazardous material or waste cleanup procedures and technologies, and coordinate the training of employees in operations, safety, environmental issues, or technical issues.
Provide expert witness testimony on issues such as soil, air, or water contamination and associated cleanup measures.

Manage site assessments or environmental studies for wind fields, including overseeing the maintenance of wind field equipment or structures, and developing processes or procedures for wind operations such as transitioning from construction to commercial operations.
Prepare requests for proposals (RFPs) for wind project construction or equipment acquisition, and prepare wind field operational budgets.
Supervise the work of subcontractors or consultants to ensure quality and conformance to specifications or budgets, and manage warranty repair or replacement services.
Track and maintain records for wind operations and maintain operations records, such as work orders, site inspection forms, or other documentation.

Provide technical support to wind field customers, employees, or subcontractors and develop relationships and communicate with customers, site managers, developers, land owners, authorities, utility representatives, or residents.
Order parts, tools, or equipment needed to maintain, restore, or improve wind field operations and estimate costs associated with operations, including repairs or preventive maintenance.
Review, negotiate, or approve wind farm contracts and recruit or select wind operations employees, contractors, or subcontractors.
Monitor and maintain records of daily facility operations and establish goals, objectives, or priorities for wind field operations.
Conduct quantitative risk assessments for human health, environmental, or other risks and identify environmental contamination sources.

Plan or implement brownfield redevelopment projects, including developing or implementing plans for the sustainable regeneration of brownfield sites to ensure regeneration of a wider area by providing environmental protection or economic and social benefits.

Prepare reports or presentations to communicate brownfield redevelopment needs, status, or progress and prepare and submit permit applications for demolition, cleanup, remediation, or construction projects.

Inspect sites to assess environmental damage or monitor cleanup progress, and coordinate on-site activities for environmental cleanup or remediation projects to ensure compliance with environmental laws, standards, regulations, or other requirements.
Design or conduct environmental restoration studies and design or implement plans for surface or ground water remediation.
Estimate costs for environmental cleanup and remediation of land redevelopment projects and develop risk management strategies based on assessment of product, compliance, or operational risks.
Develop or implement plans for revegetation of brownfield sites and coordinate the disposal of hazardous waste.
Direct the development or implementation of policies and procedures related to compliance throughout an organization and oversee internal reporting systems.
Identify project needs such as resources, staff, or finances by reviewing project objectives and schedules and communicate with key stakeholders to determine project requirements and objectives.

Use drone technology to capture high-resolution images and data for map creation and updating.
